The history of Christianity in Nigeria can be traced back to the 15th century, when the Portuguese were the first Europeans to arrive the shores of the region via the Atlantic.  The Portuguese brought Christianity with them but were unable to successfully plant Christianity because of their involvement in slave trade. It can be argued that the actual intent behind their voyage was more in the interest of slave business, than it was for missionary goals and objectives. Most of the Portuguese slave traders took Nigerian slaves to be resold in the Americas and parts of Europe. Hence, they were not committed to missionary work.

After the abolition of the slave trade in the British Empire in 1833, things took an interesting turn. Slaves captured by European masters were freed and sent to Freetown in Sierra Leone. These slaves had lived with their masters in Europe and America, and many had accepted Christianity at the time they were freed. In 1841, freed slaves arrived in Sierra Leone and gradual changes began in British policies. Slavery was frowned upon, and the British government took it upon itself to enforce new laws and policies, in a bid to stop trading and owning slaves.

Towards the end of the 19th century, some of the freed slaves began to retrace their ways back home. Freed slaves from Nigeria sailed from Sierra Leone to Lagos and Badagry on vessels acquired from slave courts in Freetown. Some of the freed slaves settled in Abeokuta (capital of Ogun State) and other cities in western Nigeria. They also had opportunities to acquire education offered by the Church Missionary Society (CMS). They practiced Christianity and preached the gospel to their family and kinsmen.  Over time, a good number of former slaves converted to Christianity. One of those slaves worthy of mention was Samuel Ajayi Crowther. He was captured at age 12 by Fulani slave raiders and sold to Portuguese slave traders. He later regained his freedom and went on to become the first African to be ordained bishop by the Christian Missionary Society (CMS) in addition to his consecration as bishop of the Niger territory. Samuel was one of the pioneers of local Christian missionary work in Nigeria and contributed to the translation of the Bible into Yoruba language.

explanation:
The Ijebu and Egba were fiercely opposed to Ibadan’s attempt to procure arms and ammunition directly from
Lagos to prosecute her wars.

explanation:
Ibadan had grown to become the most powerful Yoruba state whose administrative over other Yoruba states

punishing erring members of the royal family
defending the Kingdom from external attacks
settling disputes between the Oba and other chiefs.

the Christian missions preached against it
it became unfavourable to her industrial progress.
explanation: Following the Industrial Revolution, the European countries needed raw materials for their industries not slaves who used to provide cheap manual labour.

pave the way for the Christianization of Igboland
liberate the Igbo from Aro bondage.

further weaken the position of the elite class
assist the Christian missionaries in their activities
secure cheap labour for railway construction.
explanation:
Control of the economy would grant them unhindered access to raw materials for the European industries
and ready markets for the products from the industries.

subject: History
q: Nationalist activities developed earlier in British West Africa than in French West Africa because
options: British colonies were accorded equal status with Britain
Britain had more colonies than France
African chiefs in French colonies were highly respected
of the systems of administration adopted by the colonial powers.
explanation: The British colonial system allowed nationalist activities while the French in early stage strongly opposed it.

subject: History
q: The main reason for the construction of the railway from Port-Harcourt into the hinterland was the
options: discovery of tin in Jos area
discovery of a deep sea harbor at Port-Harcourt
need to evacuate export crops from the hinterland.
explanation: Coal is a very heavy mineral which cannot be carried by road transport

subject: History
q: During the colonial period, agricultural policy in Nigeria was designed to
options: raise the financial base of the country
make the economy compete favourably in the world market
provide raw materials for British industries
demonstrate Britain’s concern for the development of its colonies.
explanation: This was done by the encouragement of Nigerian farmers to cultivate cash crops.

a revision of the Richards Constitution
explanation:
It, among other things not listed here except the correct option, sought the establishment of a legislative house in each of the four British colonial
territories.

subject: History
q: The Lyttlelton Constitution is considered a landmark in the history of Nigeria, because it

made ministers accountable to the electorate
recognized Lagos as a federal territory
gave ministers full authority over their ministries.

rnof assembly (for legislators).

attempt by government to retire top military officers
explanation:
The crisis was exacerbated by that which resulted by the massively rigged Western Regional Election of 1965
which led to the total break-down of law and order.

subject: History
q: Major General Aguiyi-Ironsi introduced a unitary system of government because
options: it was the wish of the people
of the command structure of the military
he wanted more revenue for the central government
other military officers were planning to overthrow him.

subject: History
q: The strategy for accelerated agricultural development of the Obasanjo regime in the 1970’s focused on
options: improving transportation network to the rural areas
explanation: He aimed to achieve this through the ‘Operation Feed the Nation’ scheme..

subject: History
q: The Berber scholar, al-Mukhtar, contributed to the success of the Masina Jihad by
options: preparing the ground through the Qadiriyyah Brotherhood
mobilizing various groups to fight for Seku Ahmadu
appealing to Ardo Moudo to support Seku Ahmadu.
explanation:
He started an Islamic revival in the Niger bend and Masina thus preaching the tenets of the Quadriyyah
Brotherhood which later formed the basis for the launching of Seku Ahmadu’s Jihad.

subject: History

options: it provided an entrance to the Mediterranean.

subject: History
q: The Mahdists revolt in the Sudan was successful
options: They believed they had a superior faith
the Egyptian army was poorly trained
General Gordon was an incompetent commander.

subject: History
q: Sayyid Said increased the volume of trade with the Europeans by
options: allowing them to take control of the trade with the hinterland
allowing European consuls to reside in Zanzibar
making English the lingua franca of Zanzibar.
explanation: The European consuls were to ensure compliance with commercial treaties signed between their countries and the Omani rulers and to protect citizens of their countries from molestations.

subject: History
q: One of the main aims of the British conquest of Buganda was to
options: gain access to the cape of Good Hope
pain control of the gold mines of Bunyoro
prevent other Europeans from controlling the source of the Nile
take control of the trade routes in the area.

subject: History
q: The survival of Ethiopia as an independent polity was as a result of the
options: strong economic base of the kingdom
introduction of a compulsory military service.
explanation:
Ethiopia is characterized by highplateaux, gorges and valleys as well as
semi-desert lowlands which have
protected the country from external
invasion.

q: The Mfecane resulted in the
options: division and fragmentation of the Zulu nation
decrease in Boer influence in South Africa
dispersal of the Zulu to the Katanga copper belt.

subject: History
q: The 1960 Sharpeville revolt was specifically against
explanation: The protests were against restrictions of the movement of Africans from one area to another especially to enter any area occupied by the white people.

subject: History
q: A major consequence of the Great Trek was the
options: restriction of movement of the whites in South Africa
agitation for black-white equality in South Africa
annexation of white communities by the Africans
the expansion of white communities in South Africa.

subject: History
q: One feature of European diplomacy in the scramble for Africa was
options: negotiating with African leaders for territories
explanation:
European powers held conferences in which they signed treaties among themselves to settle disagreements
among them over rival claims laid to African territories.

subject: History

options: the African elite were opposed to it

subject: History

options: Establishment of a university in each of the colonies.
Expulsion of all Syrians and Lebanese from the colonies.
Election of Africans into each colony’s council.
Restriction of the activities of Christian missionaries in the colonies.
explanation:
The congress wanted a legislative council established for each British Colonial territory with majority of the
members being Africans.

subject: History

explanation:
The movement was led by mostly educated elite that had returned from Europe and those who took part in the
Second World War.

subject: History
q: One of the objectives for the formation of the South African Native Congress in 1912 was to
options: retrieve all lands seized by the whites
integrate the various groups and races.

subject: History
q: In 1973, Nigeria broke diplomatic relations with Israel because of Israel’s
options: support for Biafra during the war
raid on Entebbe airport in Uganda.
explanation:
Israel defeated the joint army of the six Arab nations in 1967 and again in 1973 and went ahead to annex some
important regions of Egypt.

subject: History
q: King Leopold’s colonial ventures in the Congo were aimed at
options: making Belgium a great colonial power
carving out an empire for himself
putting a stop to domestic slave trade in the area
introducing Western education to the people.
explanation:
Leopold did not want to be left out of
new territorial acquisitions in Africa following the encouragement of the French to do so by Bismarck of
Germany following the former’s loss of the rich territories of Alsace and Lorraine to the former .

subject: History
q: Increase in the number of people living below the poverty line in Africa is particularly because
options: The largest proportion of Africa's populations are unemployed
Of the problem of insecurity scaring away investors
A vast majority of its populants have chosen to be unproductive
explanation: It is largely and logically smart to trace Africa's poverty increase to the widespread unemployment rate of the youth - the largest proportion of the African population. N.B: as much as almost all of the options are right, the emphasis in the question is on 'particularly'.

subject: History
q: The First and Second World Wars were between ... respectively
explanation: The First and Second World War were between 1914-1918 and 1939-1945 respectively.

subject: History
q: The main motive behind imperialism has been aforetime categorised as primarily
options: the socio-economic transformation agenda for Africa
to expand socio-cultural territories of the Western powers
being to obtain and control a supply of raw materials for industries
explanation: The historical motivation for imperialism by the Western Powers has been to obtain and control the abundant raw materials, resources of the developing States.This particular feature of socio-economic dominion differentiates it from Colonialism as a concept.

subject: History
q: The most immediate reason for the declaration, by Ojukwu, of Eastern Nigeria as an independent State of Biafra was that
options: The Gowon-led coup strictly targeted the Igbos
The wanted the freedom for the Igbos at all cost
Of the creation of twelve States by Gowon to dismantle any secessionist agenda
corruption and oppression was too prevalent in Nigeria
explanation: Ojukwu, being Governor of the Eastern Region, immediately declared it an independence State of Biafra seeing as Gowon created twelve states in May 1976 without consultation. This, Ojukwu considered as the last straw.

subject: History
q: Igbo's active resistance to the British Colonial rule is particularly because of the
options: impression that the British officials favoured the Northerners
maladministration of the British Indirect rule Policy
explanation: The Igbos challenged the British imperial rule as the system of governance did not sit well with the egalitarian nature of their society where no one is designated out-rightly as a political leader.

subject: History
q: The Colony and Protectorate of Lagos and the Protectorate of Southern Nigeria were merged in ... to form
options: 1906, Colony and Protectorate of Southern Nigeria
1901, Colony and Protectorate of Southern Nigeria
1906, Lagos Colony and Southern Nigeria Protectorate
explanation: 1906, the Colony and Protectorate of Southern Nigeria is the correct answer. The amalgation of 1914 produced the Colony and Protectorate of Nigeria.

subject: History
q: The major administrative change made immediately after the 1914 Amalgamation was the?
options: introduction of a direct taxation system
creation of districts for political convenience
imposition of warrant chiefs in the Eastern Region
explanation: Division of Nigeria into provinces is the correct answer. The only administrative change made upon the amalgamation of 1914 was the division of the territory into provinces headed by provincial commissioners.

subject: History
q: The particular incident that led to Macaulay's imprisonment, twice, by the colonial power was
options: his criticism of British rule through the Lagos Daily News
his constant opposition to the expansion of the British rule
the humiliation of the British officials through his successful defense of Eleko in London
the attempt to silence British officials through the platform of the NNDP
explanation: In retaliation to the successful defence of Eshugbayi Eleko as the rightful king of Lagos and the humiliation this brought to the British officials, Macaulay was jailed twice by the colonial power thereafter.

subject: History
q: The London Constitutional Conference of 1957 was targeted at
options: increasing wider participation of the people in their Regional legislature
ensuring the speedy growth of federalism in Nigeria
imputing the voice of the people in their Independence Constitution
preparing ground works for the self-governance and inclusive development of Nigeria
explanation: Preparing ground works for the self-governance and inclusive development of Nigeria is the correct answer. The Conference, among other things, deliberated on the proposed dates for the independence of each of the regions, increased membership of the House of Representatives, creation of the Prime Minister's office etc for Nigeria.

subject: History
q: The colonial investments in extractive industries invariably led to the
options: urbanization of towns and cities in Nigeria
arrival of slave trading in Nigeria and Africa
motivation of a continued colonial rule
uneven development of the regions in Nigeria
explanation: The colonialists invested in raw materials and industries for their extraction employed both skilled and unskilled labour. All of these activities led to the urbanization of the vicinities around these industries.

subject: History
q: The Vice President of Nigeria during the 2nd Republic was ... under the platform of the
options: Humphrey Nwosu, National Party of Nigeria
Alex Ekwueme, National Party of Nigeria
Adisa Akinloye, Unity Party of Nigeria
Tafawa Balewa, National Party of the Nigerians and the Cameroon
explanation: Alex Ifeanyichukwu Ekwueme was the first Vice President of Nigeria in the Second Republic (1979-1983) under the platform of the National Party of Nigeria (NPN).

subject: History
q: The end of the Era of Princes in Ethiopia was a key factor in
options: decentralising state power in the hands of Provincial heads
centralising power in the hands of the Emperors of Ethiopia
motivating the Italo-Egyptian invasion of Ethiopia
explanation: The end of the Zamana Masaf∂nt (Era of Princes) led to the centralisation of state power in the hands of the Emperors of Ethiopia. This consolidation of power helped in confronting the Italo-Egyptian invasion in the 19th Century.

subject: History
q: The division of the Sokoto Caliphate into Emirates was largely due to
options: the autocratic directive of Dan Fodio
the rivalry that persisted between the Hausa Jihadists and the Fulani scholars
explanation: Geographical reasons is the correct answer. The Sokoto Caliphate because of its large size was divided into the Western Emirates under the authority of Sokoto and the Eastern Emirates which remained more or less autonomous.

subject: History
q: Decree ... was the Unitarism Policy of the Major General Aguiyi Ironsi
explanation: Decree No 34 of 1966 is the correct answer. It was labelled as the Unification Decree for the adoption of a Unitary system of government in Nigeria.

subject: History
q: All but one of the following triggered the Mahdiyya Movement in Sudan
options: the foreign government's growing reliance on European Christians
the Sudanese of governmental efforts to suppress the lucrative slave trade
unpleasant immigration policy of the foreign government
explanation: Unpleasant immigration policy of the foreign government is the correct answer. For reasons of heavy taxation which led to famine, depopulation etc, the foreign government's increased reliance on European Christians including its fight against slave trade, among related other reasons, led tp the Madhiyya revolution.

subject: History
q: One important characteristic of both recent and earlier Islamic reform movements is the emphasis on the
options: mediation of others for understanding the Koran and traditions of the prophet
use of African languages as a medium of communication
decline in the use of educational resource institutes
rigidity of the use of Arabic language
explanation: The use of African languages as a medium of communication is the correct answer. Islamic reformers, both past and present, actively support the translation of the holy texts into Swahili, Hausa, Fulfude, Wolof, Bambara and Yoruba.

subject: History
q: All the following except one were the motivating reasons behind the Great Trek
options: the fear that the emigration might lead to a decline of civilisation
insecurity about the conflict on the Eastern frontier of South Africa
restoration of economic, cultural and political unity independent of the British power
threat of liberalism by the new colonial regime
explanation: The fear that the emigration might lead to a decline of civilization is the correct answer, as this was expressed by the Dutch Reformed Church in opposition to the Great Trek, seeing also as being an act of godlessness.

subject: History
q: The central agenda of the Muhammad Ali's hegemonic rule was the
options: the exploitation of natural resources for development finance in Egypt
annexation of Sudan as an Ottoman-Egyptian Colony
explanation: Muhammad Ali and his successors sought after Sudan's natural and human resources to finance Egyptian modernizing projects, build a new army and develop agricultural and industrial enterprises.

subject: History
q: The most remarkable acheivement of the Buhari's Military regime is the
explanation: War Against Indiscipline (WAI) is the correct answer. A programme instituted in 1984 to tackle social maladjustment and widespread corruption in Nigeria.

subject: History
q: One of the following persons was not arrested over the May Day riots
explanation: Arisekola Alao is the correct answer. On May 13, 1998 under the Abacha's regime, Comrade Ola Oni, a legendary radical lecturer and activist, Chief Bola Ige, Lam Adesina were notable persons arrested over the May Day riots in Ibadan. They were arraigned before the Chief Magistrate's Court, Iyaganku, Ibadan.

subject: History
q: Imperialist powers in Africa inbuilt antagonism by taking advantage of the cultural diversity of the people through the...
explanation: The Divide and Rule Policy is the correct answer. The imperialist powers made conscious efforts to create and/or turn to its own advantage of the ethnic, cultural, tribal or religious differences within the population of their subjugated colonies. The effects of this still lingers in Nigeria and even among African countries today.

subject: History
q: ... was the only significant imperial power in the Northern half of Africa at the end of the 18th century
explanation: Ottoman Turkey is the correct answer. Its dependencies in Egypt, Tripoli, Tunis and Algiers were admittedly almost self-governing, but they did at least contribute some revenue to the sultans in Istanbul.

subject: History
q: The concern of Mary Slessor was primarily with
options: expanding the spread of colonialism under the pretext of christian missionary activities
eliminating the tribal superstitious practice of killing of triplets only
introducing the people to European culture and religion
eliminating the tribal superstitious practice of killing of twin babies
explanation: Eliminating the tribal superstitious practice of killing of twin babies is the correct answer. This was the primary concern of Mary Slessor, a Presbyterian missionary from Scotland to alter the thinking of the people as it relates to the taboo of giving birth to twin babies.

subject: History
q: The inception of state creation in the Nigerian military era is traceable to the regime of
explanation: Yakubu Gowon is the correct answer. He created 12 states for Nigeria on May, 27 1967. This initiative further placed Nigeria on the pathway to the adoption of a true federal system.

subject: History
q: Early centres of civilization in Nigeria reveal the history of
options: descendants of the notable tribes and culture
explanation: Scientific and technological developments is the correct answer. The history of the early centres of civilization presents us with the discovery and use of woods, irons and other scientific objects for the creation of notable figures, and provision of utilities in the early Nigerian societies. Notable are the Nok Culture, Ife Civilization and the Benin Crafts and Artworks.

subject: History
q: All but one of the following played vital roles in the expansion of the missionary movements into Africa
options: the regeneration of the African people
explanation: The prevalence of western education is the correct answer. The European interest in expanding missionary works into Africa are as a result of the need to introduce legitimate commerce i.e putting an end to slave trade, eliminating superstitious beliefs and practices through Christianity and civilization, etc. Western education only came as a consequence of the missionary activities.

subject: History
q: The .... was the programme initiated to institutionalise the local government system in Nigeria
explanation: The Local Government Reform of 1976 is the correct answer. It was a nationwide undertaking by the military government of Murtala/Obasanjo Regime as part of its intention to return power to the civilians and ensure active participation of the people in own affairs especially at the grassroots level.

subject: History
q: One critical factor that led to the Mfecane was the
options: decline in the growth of civilization
explanation: The war-created refugee problems is the correct answer. Refugee problems created by the wars is one of the major factors that led to the Mfecane, e.g. when Zwide attacked Sobhuza it led to the Ngwane moving their place in the Pongola valley and into an unknown region where they became invaders and raiders looking a new homeland to build the Swazi composite.

subject: History
q: The Centenary Anniversary of Nigeria was done to commemorate Nigeria's
explanation: Amalgamation of January 1st 1914 is the correct answer. On January 1st 2014, former President Ebele Jonathan inaugurated the celebration of Nigeria's Statehood and amalgation of January 1st 1914.

subject: History
q: Presently, about 65% of Africa's population is constituted by the
explanation: Youths is the correct answer. Recent statistics from the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs (UNDESA) reveals that about 65% of Africa's population is constituted by the youths.

subject: History
q: The first African female President of the United Nations General Assembly is
explanation: Angie Brooks is the correct answer. She was a Liberian diplomat and jurist, notable for being the only African female President of the United Nations General Assembly thus far.

subject: History
q: All except one of the following motivated the scramble for Africa
options: the justification that the Western Powers have the responsibilities to civilise the Non-Western Countries
an avenue for em-broadening foreign relations with Africa
the need to boost the Western Economies
that the colonies would gain access to superior European goods and influence
explanation: The imperialists were for every other reasons interested in Africa, except that they thought of it as an avenue to smoothen foreign relations with African States.

subject: History
q: The Nigerian Civil war ...
options: was fought in actual essence to reintegrate and reunify Nigeria
became inevitable because the Igbo felt that Nigeria may sooner or later divide
explanation: The Nigerian Civil War which lasted from 6th July, 1967 - 12th January, 1970 was fought as a result of the Gowon-Government's efforts to counter the struggle by the Igbos from breaking away from Nigeria. A reason for reintegration and reunification of Nigeria.

subject: History
q: King Jaja of Opobo was forcibly removed for ...
options: the attempt to declare the freedom of his people from colonial rule
explanation: In 1887, King Jaja of Opobo was forcibly removed and exiled for obstruction of commerce.

subject: History
q: The head of the administration of the Protectorate of Northern Nigeria was titled
explanation: High Commissioner is the correct answer. Lord Lugard was appointed as the High Commissioner of the Northern Protectorate in 1900.

subject: History
q: An inquiry into the need for a People-led and informed Constitution was central to ... regime
explanation: Arthur Richard had as his main priority a need to promote unity in Nigeria and ensure greater participation of the people in their own affairs. This was as a matter of fact, the surrounding essence of his 1946 Constitution.

subject: History
q: All the following except one spurred the nationalist drives in British West Africa
options: the existence of diverse political parties
the adoption of the policy of Assimilation
explanation: The adoption of the policy of Assimilation is the correct answer. This Policy was directly adopted in the French West African Colonies and it slowed down the pace of nationalism unlike in the British West African colonies.

subject: History
q: The Lyttleton Constitution is often remarkable for all the following reasons except
options: the introduction of a federal structure for Nigeria
the grant of real legislative powers to the regional legislature
recognition of the need to appoint Premiers in the regions
the establishment of a public service commission
explanation: The establishment of a public service commission is the correct answer. This was a distinct achievement of the John Macpherson's 1951 Constitution.

subject: History
q: Colonialism is often regarded as a double-edged sword particularly because
options: the infrastructure built from West African forced labour was also designed to exploit their natural resources
educational institutions were built which contributed to human capacity development
the raods built by the Europeans lead to the urbanization of West African towns and cities
the European investment in the extractive industry developed trade and commerce
explanation: Although colonial rule contributed to social development as investments and infrastructures were built, however, 'the infrastructure built from West African forced labour was also designed to exploit natural resources. A double-edged sword situation.

subject: History
q: The Nigerian Second Republic adopted a/an ... system of governance
explanation: American-styled Presidential system is the correct answer. The British Parliamentary system was jettisoned for this witnessing Alhaji Shehu Shagari as the Executive President of Nigeria.

subject: History
q: The repeated attempts by Egypt and Italy to colonize Ethiopia led to the
options: end of the Era of Princes in Ethiopia
expansion of the colonial tendencies to pacify Ethiopia
explanation: The Battle of Adwa is the correct answer. The battle was led by Emperor Menelik II of Ethiopia and the Ethiopian forces defeated the Italo-Egyptian invaders on March 1, 1896.

subject: History
q: Historically, the Yorubas came into prominence as a result of
options: their artworks and sculptures made to honour the gods
the impact of their language and cultural aspects
the settlement of the British colony in 1901
their trading in guns with the Portugese
explanation: The Yorubas who originated from Ile-Ife, arose and became quite popular and remarkable by their trading with the Portugese, which gave them a large supply of guns.

subject: History
q: Oba Ewuare of Benin Kingdom is credited with having
options: successfully managed monopoly over trade which the British found irksome
transformed Benin city into a city-state
explanation: Oba Ewuare is notable for having transformed Benin city into a City-State from a military fortress built by the Ogisos, protected by moats and walls. His era is notable, being the first Golden Age Oba.

subject: History
q: The Royal Niger Company was influential in the formation of Colonial Nigeria because it
options: enabled the British Empire to establish control over and above its German competitor
paved way for the emergence of the National African Company
served as a means of economic development and investment in Nigeria
encouraged the adoption of the Indirect Rule Policy
explanation: The Royal Niger Company enabled the British Empire to establish control over the lower Niger against the German competition led by Bismarck in the 1890s.Its company-controlled territories were made Protectorates and later amalgamated as the Colony and Protectorate of Nigeria.

subject: History
q: Which one of the following is incorrect?
options: Sarkin Fada was the head of Palace workers in the Hausa/Fulani Empire
The Ajeles properly governed the districts in the Old Oyo Empire
The Alkali Courts were headed by the Hakimis in the Hausa/Fulani Empire
The Igbo precolonial era was acephalous in nature
explanation: The Alkali Courts were headed by the Alkali judges in the Hausa/Fulani Empire not the Hakimis, who were actually the head of districts.

subject: History
q: Under the chairmanship of ... the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) was formed
explanation: Yakubu Gowon is the correct answer. West African leaders under the chairmanship of President Yakubu Gowon, met in Lagos in May 1975 to create the ECOWAS.

subject: History
q: Unarguably, the abolition of the slave trade in Britain directly stimulated the
options: penetration of European merchants capital into Nigeria
spread of missionary activities into Nigeria
growth of nationalist movements in Nigeria
pace of the amalgamation of Nigeria
explanation: Penetration of European merchants capital into Nigeria is the correct answer. As slave trade was abolished, legitimate trade started and this encouraged the European merchants agenda for imperialism (i.e to establish industries, extract and trade natural resources) to smoothly penetrate Nigeria.

subject: History
q: The connections between Kano and the province of Nupe was notable in the reign of Kano's
explanation: Sarkin Dauda is the correct answer. In the reign of Kano's Sarkin Dauda (c. 1421-38), the connection between Kano and Nupe was visible and the major power between them was Zaria, which conquered a large expanse of land.

subject: History
q: In 17th Century, the Dutch settlers largely relied on ... to build up South Africa
explanation: Black South African slaves is the correct answer. Apartheid system was rooted in the enslavement of the black South Africans as instruments to build up South Africa. This was in the 17th Century. At the end of slave trade in 1863, the base was shifted to the discovery of diamonds and gold.

subject: History
q: A Policy central to the Kanem-Borno Empire was that of
explanation: Imperial expansion and firearms trading is the correct answer. Kanem-Borno Empire believed in the expansion of its imperial territories and in the same vein, traded in firearms and horses for the purpose of creating a sophisticated cavalry.

subject: History
q: All the following except one led to the re-emergence of old trade patterns under Omani rule
options: attack on the Portuguese in the 1640s
willingness of Africans in the interior to take slaves to the coast
explanation: Attack on the Portugese in the 1649s is the correct answer as this attack was carried as a revenge on the Portuguese who had sought to control their strategic position of the Straits of Hormuz at the entrance of the Persian Gulf.

subject: History
q: In Sierra Leone, the ... was the first established Christian Church for British freed slaves among others
explanation: The Church Missionary Society (CMS) is the correct answer. The CMS, an offshoot of the Anglican Church was the first to become firmly established mainly among the freed slave settlers from Britain, Nova, Scotia and other parts of the New World.

subject: History
q: One critical factor which spurred the abrupt termination of the Nigerian fledging democracy in the First Republic was the
options: urge for sectional military representation in government
use of the soldiers to quell civil unrests and riots
explanation: The correct answer is the nationwide corruption by the politicians. This gave the military coup plotters the leverage to overthrow the ruling government. N.B: The question tests your knowledge on what most importantly inspired the military disruption of the Nigerian growing democracy in the 1st Republic.

subject: History
q: The Mahdiyya Movement in Sudan overthrew the ...
explanation: Ottoman-Egyptian Regime in the early 1880s is the correct answer. In Sudan, the Mahdiyya Movement was the revolutionary movement that overthrew the Ottoman-Egyptian regime which conquered Sudan in 1820-1822.

subject: History
q: ... has primarily led to the growth of Islamic reform movements in many sub-Saharan African countries
options: The crisis of modernisation of the 20th Century
The new discoveries on the inadequacies of Islam
The shift in the belief and religious system of Islam
The rigidity in the use of Arabic language
explanation: The crisis of modernisation of the 20th century is the correct answer. Islamic reform movements developed majorly in response to the challenges of social development (e.g. the Mosques are often known as platforms for criticism of the shortcomings of the African government regimes), educational reform (e.g. training as religious scholars), and the popularisation of Islamic values.

subject: History
q: One of the following is attributable to the Great Trek
options: the expansion of white communities in South Africa
the agitation for black-white equality in South Africa
the annexation of white communities by the Africans
restriction of movement of the whites in South Africa
explanation: The expansion of white communities in South Africa is the correct answer. The Great Trek was a movement of Dutch-speaking colonists up into the interior of Southern Africa in search of land to establish their own homeland, independent of British rule.

subject: History
q: Khedive Ismail appointed British officers in Sudan in order to
explanation: Fight against lucrative slave trade is the correct answer. The British officers - Samuel Baker and Charles Gordon were appointed by Khedive Ismail in order to spread Ottoman-Egyptian control over the upper reaches of the White Nile and fight against the slave trade.

subject: History
q: The ... headed under the Chairmanship Muhammadu Buhari was the governing body in Nigeria
explanation: The Supreme Military Council is the correct answer. The Provisional Ruling Council was adopted by Abacha and the Armed Forces ruling Council by Babangida.

subject: History
q: The National Democratic Coalition (NADECO) was formed
options: in 1993, to press for the re-validation of the June 12, 1993 Presidential election
in 1994, to solicit funds in preparation for the transition into Civilian rule
in 1994, to press for the re-validation of the June 12, 1993 Presidential election
in 1993, to advocate for the violation of human rights under the Abacha's regime
explanation: The correct answer is May 15, 1994, the NADECO was established to press for the revalidation of the June 12, 1993 Presidential election and the reinstallation of all democratic structures.

subject: History
q: All the following except one are the communes of the colony of Senegal where the Policy of Assimilation was applied
explanation: Kaolack is the correct answer. The four communes of the then colony of Senegal where the Policy of Assimilation were extended include Saint Louis, Goree, Rofisque and Dakar.

subject: History
q: The system of imperial military rule had its deepest roots in ...
explanation: Egypt is the correct answer. The Ottoman Power conquered the Sultanate in 1517 and took over as the operating institution since the 13th century. Upon this, the system of military rule had its deepest roots in Egypt.

subject: History
q: Traditionally, the Efik people have accounts of their migration from the territory
explanation: Ibibio and Igbo is the correct answer. Although the actual origins of the Efik people are unknown. However, historical oral traditions provide accounts of their migration from Igbo and Ibibio territory (to the North-west of Calabar) to their present location.

subject: History
q: Regionalism notable with ... may be said to have laid the foundational basis for the latter state creation in Nigeria
explanation: Authur Richard is the correct answer. The 1946 Richard Constitution established three regional councils, one each in the North, East and West. This historically forms one of the basic foundational existence upon the Nigerian Federal structure relied subsequently in terms of states creation during the military era.

subject: History
q: The Ife Civilization is most remarkable for its
options: use of terra cotta and bronze heads
its discovery of terra cotta (burnt clay)
use of carved woods and ivory
explanation: Use of terra cotta and bronze heads is the correct answer. Objects such as stools and figures were carved on hard stone called quartz while animal and humanity figures were carved from granite and decorated with iron nails.

subject: History
q: Baptists, Methodists and the ... were among the first to engage in the Liberiian Christian Mission
explanation: Episcopal Church is the correct answer. The Baptists, Methodists and Episcopalians were among the first to engage in Liberian Mission as a result of the missionary fervour that gripped Western Europe and America in the 19th Century.

subject: History
q: The Swazi composite is the modern ...
explanation: Swaziland is the correct answer. The Swazi composite is also now what modern Swaziland is, which means that some modern day countries in Southern and Central Africa can trace their roots to the Mfecane.

subject: History
q: One of the following is not credited with the General Obasanjo's military regime
options: the Festival of Arts and Culture
explanation: The Structural Adjustment programme is the correct answer. It was a Programme inaugurated during the Ibrahim Babangida's regime.

subject: History
q: The Colony and Protectorate of Nigeria was the resultant merger of the
options: Colony of Lagos and the Southern Protectorate
Colony and Protectorate of Southern Nigeria and the Northern Protectorate
Northern Protectorate and the Colony of Western Nigeria
Colony of Western Nigeria and the Protectorate of Southern and Northern Nigeria
explanation: Colony and Protectorate of Southern Nigeria and the Northern Protectorate were the constituents of the area that later became Colony and Protectorate of Nigeria upon the amalgamation of Lord Lugard on January 1st 1914.

subject: History
q: The maasai tribe of East African speaks ...... language among the Nilotic family of African tribes.
explanation: Maa is one of the languages of the Nilotic tribe, other languages include Kanuri, Fur, Songhay, Nobiin and so on.

subject: History
q: The policy of assimilation fails in French colony mainly due to _________
options: no respect for the peoples opinion
explanation: The French policy of assimilation has no regard for the people's culture unlike the indirect rule system of British in West Africa.

subject: History
q: __________ were the first set of people to land on the present day Nigeria territory for trading activities.
explanation: The Portuguese were the first to do so, establishing trade with the Benin kingdom, Lagos and other regions along the coast.

subject: History
q: The _________ wield the administrative power over other Yoruba land in 19th century.
explanation: The Oyo empire was a vast empire/territory, which happens to be the most popular and outstanding of all empires or kingdoms in Yoruba political system.

subject: History
q: The Benin kingdom is stratified into three age grades which are
explanation: In Benin kingdom, land is owned by the villages and each village is divided into three grades which are Irogbae ( between age 0-16), Igbele ( unmarried people that form part of warriors) Edion ( old people who has achieved status in the society).

subject: History
q: The method of tax collection introduced by the warrant chiefs in the Eastern Nigeria brought about __________
explanation: Prior to the advent of British domination, the Igbo society is acephalous in nature and has no clear-cut government. The warrant chief system which was later introduced to them (Igbo) brought panic, part of which is the Aba women riot of 1929/30.

subject: History
q: Egyptian nationalism in late 18th century and early 20th century majorly aimed at ________
options: turning out military dictatorship in Egypt
explanation: Egypt, who finally got her independence in 1922 in her 19th century was seen fighting colonialism through her nationalists to ensure independence.

subject: History
q: The Emir of Sokoto controls the ________ empire as one of the supreme headquarter.
explanation: The sokoto caliphate was one of the two supreme headquarter, it controls the Eastern empire and headed by an Emir. The Eastern empire include Yauri.

subject: History
q: One main factor that led to depopulation of Nigerians around 1715 to 1789 was?
explanation: Depopulation is the destruction or expulsion of inhabitants from their land. The Trans-Atlantic slave trade brought depopulation to numbers of Nigeria inhabitants in 18th century.

subject: History
q: The establishment of _________ by ______ help fast track the activity of nationalist in Nigeria
explanation: Herbert Macaulay Olayinka Samuel was a Nigerian politician and generally referred to as father of Nigeria nationalism, he established the first newspaper in Nigeria in 1923 called Lagos daily news

subject: History
q: The first capital of Kanem-Bornu was located at ________
explanation: Kanem-Bornu was probably founded around the mid-9th century, and it's first capital was at Njimi, northern-east of the lake.

subject: History
q: The 1959 general election was between three political parties which at the time were
explanation: The three (3) main political parties that participated in the 1959 election were AG, NCNC and NPC, there was no clear winner, making NCNC form coalition government with NPC leaving AG as watchdog.

subject: History
q: __________ was who formed the name Nigeria after the amalgamation of Southern protectorate and Northern protectorate.
explanation: Flora Shaw who was a journalist, and later became the wife of the first governor - general of Nigeria, gave the name to Nigeria due to the Niger river that pass across the land.

subject: History
q: Most Omani belongs to ________ muslim faith and are regarded as the followers of the Abdullah Ibn Ibad
explanation: Oman is the only country in the muslim world with an Ibadi-Majority population.

subject: History
q: Before the fall of apartheid and expansion of franchise to all residents of the country, Nelson Mandela was imprisoned for
explanation: Nelson Mandela was imprisoned in 1962, and subsequently sentenced to a life imprisonment for conspiring to overthrow the state. Mandela served 27 years in Jail.

subject: History
q: The trans-Atlantic slave trade was displace with search for raw materials by the British due to ___________
explanation: Due to the need for raw material (industrial revolution), the slave business was suppressed.

subject: History
q: The spread of Islam across East Africa was a major impact of the _________
explanation: Omani is a country on the southwest coast of the Arabian peninsula in Western Asia. Their (Oman) major influence was the spread of Islam in East Africa.

subject: History
q: The second military coup d' e tat in Nigeria took place on
explanation: The July coup, or the so called 'July Rematch' was the second of the many military coups in Nigeria

subject: History
q: One of the following is generally referred to as the first executive president in Nigeria
explanation: President Shehu Shagari became the first executive president in Nigeria after being successfully handed power to by the Olusegun Obasanjo military regime.

subject: History
q: The Mahdist rebel against the Egyptian authority was a success due to
options: A fight back from every citizen
explanation: Due to the widespread support of the Mahdist revolt, it became successful at the end from Egyptian rule.

subject: History
q: Nigeria became the ________ member of United nation organization in 1960.
explanation: United Nation Organization created in 1945, which foster on world peace and security has 193 members including Nigeria, who was her 99th.

subject: History
q: Sierra Leone finally got freedom from the British colonial domination in ________
explanation: Sierra Leone, one of the British west Africa colony, got her independence on 27th April, 1961 under the leadership of Sir Milton Margai who became their first prime minister.

subject: History
q: _________ led to the European merchant agenda for imperialism and smooth penetration of Nigeria
options: The need to extend christianity in Africa
Abolition of slave trade and the need for raw materials.
Need to help Nigeria become federation
explanation: Abolition of slave trade cause the British capitalist to exploit foreign countries in order to get raw material to develop their industry.

subject: History
q: One of these is not a social development under colonial rule.
explanation: Exploitation is a negative impact of colonialism e.g Lever Brothers took over the Niger company in 1929, they became heirs to one of the most notorious explorers of the 19th century African.

subject: History
q: The Muslim reform movement have grown due to inspiration from all the following except
explanation: The movement have often been inspired by North Africans, Arabs or in some cases, Indo-Pakistani traditions of reform, such as the salafiya of Muhammed Abduh.

subject: History
q: The support of the Felahin helps Mohammed Ali ___________
explanation: Fellahin are native peasants or labourer, mostly farmers or agricultural labourers in the middle east and north Africa, they supported Mohammed Ali gain effective control of Egypt.

subject: History
q: The protectorate of Nigeria in 1914 became _________ in 1946
explanation: Protectorates in 1914 became regions in 1946, under Arthur Richard constitution.

subject: History
q: Ethiopia's lowlands and high plateau protected the country from ___________
explanation: Ethiopia is located on a semi - desert lowlands, valleys and gorges which protected the country from foreign invasion.

subject: History
q: One of the following is not a gearing factor towards the great trek.
options: Bringing back to normal the economic, cultural and political independence
The panic that emigration might decline civilization
The fear of conflict on the eastern frontier of South Africa
explanation: The Dutch reformed church was in opposition to great trek, seeing it also as being an act of godlessness

subject: History
q: The decrease of Boer influence in South Africa was as a result of
explanation: Mfecane was the period of widespread chaos and warfare among indigenous ethnic communities in South Africa

subject: History
q: The main economic activity of the Nupe people is
explanation: The Nupe people are called different name by various tribe in Nigeria, e.g Yoruba refer them as Takpa. The Nupe are noted throughout Nigeria for glass beads, fine leather and mat work, brass trays, and fine cloth.

subject: History
q: The crown colony system is ________
options: a form of British direct rule of British annexed territory
the home of the queen for admin purpose
where all the African countries are administered
the seat for traditional rulers playing indirect rule
explanation: The crown colony system is British annexed territories in which the British established political institution such as the executive council, legislative council and so on

subject: History
q: The Murtala/Obasanjo's regime _________
options: had a programme called war against discipline
was the pioneered of the structural adjustment programme
favoured decolonization and self determination of Africa states
explanation: The regime favours decolonization, a case in point was event in Angola where the regime support MPLA, Augustino Neto's popular movement for the liberation of Angola from UNITA.

subject: History
q: The Efik serve as ___________ between the white trader on the coast and the inland tribes of Cross river.
explanation: The Efik were the middle men between the white traders on the coast and the inland tribes of the Cross river and Calabar district.

subject: History
q: United Nation Organization emerge as a result of collapse of ____________
explanation: League of Nations was created in 1919 to prevent the outbreak of another world war after that of 1914-18.

subject: History
q: The mid western region was created out of the ___________ in 1963.
explanation: The Mid western region was created in 1963 out of the existing western region, and later in 1976 known as the Bendel state

subject: History
q: The discovery of Terracotta in 1928 on NOK soil in Kaduna state was by?
explanation: The Terracotta was accidentally unearthed at a level of 24 feet from an alluvial tin mine.

subject: History
q: The three existing groups before the creation of Organizations of Africa Unity in 1963 were __________
explanation: These were the former blocs that were existing before the creation of Organization of Africa Unity on May 25, 1963 with headquarters at Addis Abbaba.

subject: History
q: The first Africa to be ordained bishop by the Christian missionary school in addition to consecration as bishop of the Niger territory is ___________
explanation: Ajayi Crowther was a captured slave at age 12 by the fulani slave raiders and sold to Portuguese slave traders, he later regained his freedom and became first to be ordained Bishop.

subject: History
q: The strained relationship between Nigeria and Britain during Murtala/Obasanjo's regime in 1975 led to ________
options: nationalisation of British petroleum and Barclays bank
nationalisation of cocoa house in Ibadan
explanation: The correct answer is - the nationalisation of British petroleum and Barclays bank. Due to the support Nigeria gave MPLA, Augustino Neto's popular movement for liberation of Angola against UNITA, there was a strain reaction from Britain and some other western countries.

subject: History
q: In whose regime was illegal West Africa aliens living in Nigeria expelled?
explanation: About one million illegal aliens were expelled in 1975, the land borders were closed to check smuggling and the inflow of illegal immigrants.

subject: History
q: The Abacha's regime adopted a _______ posture with the international organizations.
explanation: The Abacha's administration is accused of abuse of human rights, and subsequently sanctions were clamped down on the country from the west and the member nations of commonwealth etc.

subject: History
q: _______ who was the minister of defence staff of the republic of Biafra, formally renounced the existence of the state in 1970.
explanation: The Nigeria civil war started on July 6, 1967 and come to an end on January 15, 1970 when Philip Effiong ceased fire with Yakubu gowon.

subject: History
q: One of the following is regarded as the founding father of the Kanuri?
explanation: The old Nupe kingdom was established in the middle of 15th century in a basin between the Niger and Kaduna river, in what is now central Nigeria and the legendary founder of Nile is Ali Ghaji.

subject: History
q: The regional south Africa in Africa comprises of _________ countries.
explanation: South Africa is one of the 5 regions of Africa. South Africa is then further subdivided into 11 independent countries which include Republic of South Africa.

subject: History
q: Portuguese speaking countries in Africa who were formerly colonized by Portugal are generally refer to as____________
explanation: A Busophone country is that which was formerly colonized by Portugal and speaks Portuguese as her official language, E.g Carpe Verde, Guinea Bissau and Mozambique.

subject: History
q: The method of order used by Europeans to take over the present day Nigeria is________
options: Trader, colonialist, indirect rule and freedom
explanation: Most European groups that came to Nigeria started as an explorer, then later trading with the locals, and sometimes after missionaries set in and finally the imperialists motive ( Foreigners dominating domestic economic).

subject: History
q: Once an empty calabash is send out by the Bashorun to an unconstitutional Alaafin on behalf of the Oyomesis, such Alaafin has to ________
explanation: An empty calabash could be sent by Bashorun ( leader of Oyomesi) to Alaafin who has acted unconstitutional, this message authorize such Alaafin to commit suicide.

subject: History
q: Every citizens of Benin kingdom is an Oba's subject and were regarded as ________
explanation: The political organization of the Benin kingdom (Bini) sees Oba as a mighty leader, who all citizens are subjected to.

subject: History
q: The Clifford constitution is considered as a landmark in Nigeria history, because it ________
options: recognized Lagos as a federal territory
Pave way for the office of Prime minister
explanation: Clifford constitution for the first time established elective principle, this is that, election was conducted into the legislative council.

subject: History
q: North Africa according to United Nation Classification comprises of how many countries.
explanation: The UN sub - region of North Africa consist of 7 countries which are Algeria, Egypt, Libya, Morocco, Sudan, Tunisia, Western Sahara.

subject: History
q: One of the following majorly influence the success of Sokoto jihad
options: lack of Unity among Hausa states
the mighty men of the Jihadist
explanation: The Sokoto jihad which happened in 1804, was a success mainly because of lack of unity among the Hausa state. Othman Dan Folio, the leader of the war then established the Sokoto caliphate with outstanding centralize political system.

subject: History
q: The South-Eastern part of Nigeria was _________ in its political system before the advent of colonialism.
explanation: The south-eastern part of Nigeria also know as Igbo society is an highly decentralized administration, in other words, they didn't have a single ruler or overall ruler administering the community

subject: History
q: One but all are factors that lead to growth of nationalism in Nigeria.
explanation: All the answers except option D gave rise to Nigeria nationalism to stand up as one against British Colonial rule.

subject: History
q: ____________ played a prominent role in the government of the Kanem-Borno empire.
explanation: Magira, queen mother alongside Gumsu ( Mai's first wife) performed important activities in the palace and they trained the princes. They are great influence in the politics of the empire.

subject: History
q: ___________ constitution gave full blown to federalism in Nigeria.
explanation: The Oliver Lyttleton constitution of 1954 grant Nigeria a formal and well recognised federal structure, this is that the power of the country is well decentralized as it should properly be.

subject: History
q: Lagos was annexed and became British colony in _________
explanation: Lagos, now part of the Southern Nigeria was annexed on 6th of August 1861 under the treat of force by Commander Beddingfield of HMS Prometheus, who was accompanied by the acting British consul, William's McCoskry.

subject: History
q: The main party against apartheid party in South African was the __________
explanation: The ANC was formed on 8th January, 1912, and has fight against apartheid with all its power, it has been the ruling party of post-apartheid South Africa since election of Nelson Mandela in the 1994 election.

subject: History
q: All but one is not impact of British on Nigeria.
explanation: The Islamic law also known as Sharia law, is a Muslim law and was introduced to the northerners of Nigeria by Arabs even before the advent of British domination.

subject: History
q: The Scramble of Africans by Europeans in 1884/85 Berlin Conference made Nigeria a British _________ colony.
explanation: Nigeria is one of the British west African colonies, others include Ghana, Gambia, and Sierra Leone.

subject: History
q: Before 1963 __________ was the highest court in Nigeria
explanation: The Supreme court become the highest court in 1963, but before then, the Privy council in London sees to dispute settlement and matters adjudication.

subject: History
q: The Coup that took Shehu Shagari out from office was a __________ coup.
explanation: President Shehu Shagari was removed from office through palace coup by Mohammed Buhari on January 1, 1983.

subject: History
q: The Mahdist state or Sudanese Mahdiyya, was a _________ movement launched in 1881.
explanation: The Sudanese Mahdiyyaq is a religious and political movement by Muhammad Ahmed bin Abdullah (later Muhammad al-Mahdi) against the Khediavate of Egypt, which had ruled the Sudan since 1821.

subject: History
q: The Nigeria president who with Eyadema of Togo spearhead the formation of ECOWAS is ___________
explanation: The Economic Community of West Africa State (ECOWAS) founded on 28th May, 1975 in Lagos was spearheaded by President Eyadema of Togo and Yakubu Gowon of Nigeria.

subject: History
q: Freetown was use to facilitate ________ from 1822 to early 20th century.
explanation: Freetown, was founded as home for repatriated former slaves in 1787, it has a special significance in the history of the trans-Atlantic slave trade as a departure point for thousands of West African captives.

subject: History
q: __________ is the economic control and exploitation of foreign lands arising from necessity to reverse the impediment to the accumulation of capital.
explanation: Imperialism is an outward drive of certain people to build empires, colonies and to occupy privileges position in market of foreign lands.

subject: History
q: Due to colonialism, the new day Nigeria now has _______as her official language.
explanation: English became the official language of Nigeria since colonial period and this has helped the country stay unified. It (English) gave the people better social interaction despite the diverse of cultures in the land.

subject: History
q: The point where the recent and earlier Islamic reform movement relate is the __________
options: use of Arabic language as strict medium
support of holy Quran translation to African languages
decline in the use of educational resources institutes
need for terrorist group to take over world on Islamic ground
explanation: The recent and earlier Islamic movement actively support the translation of the holy text into Swahili, Hausa, Fulfude, Wolof, Bambara and Yoruba.

subject: History
q: ___________ was the governor of the protectorate of Lagos and Southern Nigeria in 1906.
explanation: Sir Walter Egerton had a long career in the administration of British empire, holding senior position which included the governorship of Lagos colony and southern Nigeria in 1906.

subject: History
q: The main reason Liberia and Ethiopia was not colonised is
options: The former is a strong country with thriving empire while the later is a country for freed American slave.
The latter is a strong country with thriving empire while the former is a country for freed American slave.
The former is a Christian made nation while the latter is a mixed giving it freedom from colonial lord
The latter surrender fast while the former surrender later
explanation: The Italian had attempted many times to colonize Ethiopia, but all attempts failed due to Ethiopia military strength and location, while Liberia was formed as a home for freed American slaves

subject: History
q: The expansion of white communities in South Africa is a major cause of ________
explanation: The great trek starting in 1836 in southern Africa, was a mass migration of Dutch-speaking inhabitants of British run Cape colony, who left the Cape and travelled eastward by wagon train to the interior of the continent to live beyond the reach of British colonial rule.

subject: History
q: Mfecane in South Africa happened between ____________
explanation: Mfecane happened in Southern Africa during the period between 1815 to 1840, thus, easing the way for white expansion into Natal and the Highveld.

subject: History
q: One of the following is not feature of the traditional economic activities of the Yoruba people.
explanation: The Yoruba people are the current south-west inhabitant in Nigeria, they are popularly known for their farming lifestyle among other things.

subject: History
q: Lagos colony and protectorate of southern Nigeria was merge merged together to form ____________
options: Lagos colony and protectorate of southern Nigeria
explanation: For British administration of current day Nigeria to move fast, they gradually amalgamate the three different administrative units, starting with Lagos colony and protectorate of southern Nigeria.

subject: History
q: The first regime to successfully transfer power back to civil rule was
explanation: Murtala/Obasanjo was the first to successfully transfer power back to civilian in 1979 after the constitution drafting committee, headed by chief Rotimi Williams, submitted the drafted constitution to the president and has been reviewed by the constituent assembly, headed by Justice Udo Udomma.

subject: History
q: The Efik are primarily located in _________
explanation: The Efik are sub-ethnic group of Ibibio located primarily in southern Nigeria, in the southern part of Cross River state

subject: History
q: One of these is not an organ of Organisation of Petroleum exporting countries.
explanation: The General Assembly is an of organ of the United Nations and not of the Organisation of Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC).

subject: History
q: The completion of the 36 states in Nigeria was done by ____________ in __________ year
explanation: The completion of 36 state in Nigeria was by Abacha on October 1, 1996.

subject: History
q: The civilization of NOK people depict
options: The different stages of development in Nigeria
explanation: The NOK, through archaeological research has pass through early, middle and late stone age as well as well as stone age.

subject: History
q: The fast spread of Christianity in Nigeria is associated with
explanation: After the abolition of slave trade in 1833 by Britain, many were freed to sierra leone and some trace their root back to Nigeria, they also had opportunity to acquire education offered by the Church Missionary Society (CMS)

subject: History
q: After the death of Said bin Sultan, the empire was divided in what year and they practised what type of governmnent?
explanation: 1856 was when the empire was divided between among his sons into two sultanates. They practiced a monarchical system because they have a traditional ruler known as Said Bin Sultan.

subject: History
q: The method that was used by the Europeans to control the admininstration of the Southern Nigeria was
explanation: Divide and Rule was used to control the Southern Nigeria by encouraging dissent among them, thereby preventing them from uniting in opposition.

subject: History
q: The Ogboni cult in the Yoruba pre colonial Adminstration played the role of
explanation: The Ogboni is the third organ of government of the old Oyo empire. Among their functions is to check the excesses use of power by the Oyo mesi. It i also their duty to resolve cases between the Alaafin and the Oyo mesi.

subject: History
q: Imperialism was adopted by Europeans to
options: develop the economies of the colonies
expand its economy and political base
establish a democratic society similar to that of Europe
end ethnic rivalry in the colonies
explanation: The Europeans colonized other countries (mostly African contries) to get economic resources to expand their economic and political tentacles.

subject: History
q: The indirect rule system succeeded in Huasa/Fulani society because the
explanation: The Emirs were made use of in the adminsitration to control their society with their own already existing customs and traditions.

subject: History
q: The system of government in the Benin pre colonial system of government is __________
explanation: The Benin were ruled by The Oba, monarch (traditional ruler) and political leader of the empire with absolute authority no one dares to challenge.

subject: History
q: The most important conquest of Umar was that they establish the __________
explanation: Tijaniyah is an order of path originating in the Maghren but now wide spread into the Northern part of Sudan. One of Umar's achievement was establishing the Tijaniyah.

subject: History
q: All of these are independent Hausa city states in Nigeria except
explanation: Daura is the second independent Hausa city states out of seven. The city states include: Biram, Daura, Kano, Katsina, Kano and Zaria.

subject: History
q: The division of Sokoto caliphates into emirates is due to ________
options: the autocratic rule of Dan Fodio
explanation: It was divided into various emirates (emirates system) which each emirate will be headed by an Emir.

subject: History
q: The Nationalist organization formed in Nigeria in the late 1930s was the
options: National Council of Nigeria and the Cameeroons
explanation: The NYM was the first nationalist movement with national colourization and it has some prominient members in it, the likes of Obafemi Awolowo, Ernest Ikoli etc

subject: History
q: The Igbo's active resistance to British colonial rule is particaularly because of the

subject: History

This policy was emphasized by the French between 1920-1945.

subject: History
q: The Berlin conference on the partitioning of Africa was held in _______.
explanation: In 1884 at the request of Portugal, German Chancellor Otto von Bismark called together the major western powers of the world to negotiate questions and end confusion over the control of Africa. The conference lasted until February 26, 1885 – a three month period where colonial powers haggled over geometric boundaries in the interior of the continent, disregarding the cultural and linguistic boundaries already established by the Native Indigenous African population. What ultimately resulted was a hodgepodge of geometric boundaries that divided Africa into fifty irregular countries.

subject: History
q: The indigenous pre-colonial Nigerian economy consisted of the following except
explanation: The indigenous pre-colonial Nigerian economy consisted of agriculture, local industry and crafts, trade and services. In modern Yoruba history, it is a foremost document that was deliberately designed to foster unity and peace among all parts of Yoruba land and among the entire Yoruba nation, Black Africa's largest nation and Black Africa's foremost leader in civilization.

subject: History

subject: History

A strong mercantile relationship developed, with the Edo trading slaves and tropical products such as ivory, pepper and palm oil for European goods such as manillas and guns.

subject: History

subject: History

explanation: Mohammad Ali is regarded as the father of modern Egypt. He founded the Khedivate of Egypt and Sudan in 1805, capitalizing on the disorder in Egypt that followed the French occupation (1798 to 1801), and his descendants ruled Egypt until the Free Officers' Revolution in 1952.

subject: History

explanation: After the death of Said bin Sultan in 1856 the empire was divided between his sons into two sultanates, an African section (Sultanate of Zanzibar) ruled by Majid bin Said and an Asian section (Sultanate of Muscat and Oman) ruled by Thuwaini bin Said.

subject: History

explanation: The Mahdist War was a war between the Mahdist Sudanese of the religious leader Muhammad Ahmad bin Abd Allah, who had proclaimed himself the "Mahdi" of Islam, and the forces of the Khedivate of Egypt, initially, and later the forces of Britain.

subject: History
q: Who was the first state president of the republic of South Africa?
explanation: The Republic of South Africa was proclaimed on 21 May 1961. Charles Robberts Swart, the last Governor-General, was sworn in as the first State President. The title 'State President' was originally used for the head of state of the Boer Republics, and like them, the holder of the office wore a sash with the Republic's coat of arms. He was elected to a seven-year term by the Parliament of South Africa, and was not eligible for re-election.

subject: History
q: The Trans-Atlantic slave trade was abolished in Britain in what year?
explanation: Britain finally abolished the trans-Atlantic slave trade in 1807, and the United States implemented its ban a year later in 1808. Despite these legal bans, and subsequent acts to suppress the trade in the United States and elsewhere, the illegal trans-Atlantic slave trade continued into the 1860s.

subject: History
q: When was the Census crisis in Nigeria?
explanation: The 1962 population census put the total population of the country at 45 million, out of which the south had 24 million and the north 21 million. This was the first time the population of the south would exceed that of the north. The population count created a big political problem for the Balewa government, which had to cancel it and order a re-count.

subject: History
q: When did Christianity start in Sierra Leone?
explanation: Christianity was first introduced in Sierra Leone in 1792, however, Sierra Leone is a predominately Muslim country today. Seventy-eight percent of the population are Muslim, and the country's two largest ethnic groups, the Mende and Temne, are Muslim majority.

subject: History
q: Islam came to Nigeria in the ______ century.
explanation: The spread of Islam in Nigeria dates back to the eleventh century when it first appeared in Borno in the northeast of the country. Later Islam emerged in Hausaland in the northwest and its influence was evident in Kano and Katsina.

subject: History

subject: History
q: Which of these was not an effect of the Great Trek?
options: Afrikaners remained politically divided for many years
The Trek resulted in the cultural and economic isolation of the Boers.
The Great Trek led directly to the founding of several autonomous Boer republics
It reduced trade between black and white groups
explanation: The Great Trek led directly to the founding of several autonomous Boer republics, namely the South African Republic (also known simply as the Transvaal), the Orange Free State, and the Natalia Republic. As a result of the Trek, the Afrikaners remained politically divided for many years. Furthermore, the Trek resulted in the cultural and economic isolation of the Boers. The Great Trek increased the conflicts between the Boers and indigenous tribes, but, on the other hand stimulated trade between black and white groups.

subject: History

subject: History

subject: History

subject: History

subject: History
q: Who is the father of nationalism in Nigeria?
explanation: Nigerian nationalism is territorial nationalism and emphasizes a cultural connection of the people to the land, particularly the Niger and the Benue Rivers. It first emerged in the 1920s under the influence of Herbert Macaulay, who is considered to be the founder of Nigerian nationalism.

subject: History

explanation: The British conquest of Egypt (1882), also known as Anglo-Egyptian War occurred in 1882 between Egyptian and Sudanese forces under Ahmed 'Urabi and the United Kingdom. It ended a nationalist uprising against the Khedive Tewfik Pasha. It established firm British influence over Egypt at the expense of the Egyptians, the French, and the Ottoman Empire, whose already weak authority became nominal.

subject: History

In Nigeria in 1943, a visitor came to archaeologist Bernard Fagg and drew his attention to some unique-looking artifacts, which Fagg and his colleagues eventually determined belonged to a then-unknown culture now known as the Nok.

Forty-six years later in 1960, Nigeria became an independent state.

subject: History

explanation: Great Trek, Afrikaans Groot Trek, the emigration of some 12,000 to 14,000 Boers from Cape Colony in South Africa between 1835 and the early 1840s, in rebellion against the policies of the British government and in search of fresh pasturelands.
